# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Main Deno Deploy application with dynamic module loading plugin system

// Plugin interface that loaded modules should implement
interface Plugin {
  name: string;
  version?: string;
  initialize?: () => Promise<void> | void;
  handle?: (request: Request) => Promise<Response | null> | Response | null;
  transform?: (data: unknown) => Promise<unknown> | unknown;
}

// Plugin registry to keep track of loaded plugins
const pluginRegistry: Map<string, Plugin> = new Map();
const pluginErrors: Map<string, string> = new Map();

// Load a plugin from a module path
async function loadPlugin(modulePath: string): Promise<Plugin | null> {
  try {
    // Validate the module path
    if (!isValidModulePath(modulePath)) {
      throw new Error(`Invalid module path: ${modulePath}`);
    }

    // Dynamic import of the module
    const module = await import(modulePath);

    // Check if the module exports a default plugin or a plugin object
    const plugin: Plugin = module.default || module.plugin || module;

    if (!plugin.name) {
      // Use the module path as a fallback name
      plugin.name = modulePath.split("/").pop()?.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, "") || modulePath;
    }

    // Initialize the plugin if it has an initialize method
    if (typeof plugin.initialize === "function") {
      await plugin.initialize();
    }

    pluginRegistry.set(plugin.name, plugin);
    console.log(`Plugin loaded successfully: ${plugin.name} (${modulePath})`);

    return plugin;
  } catch (error) {
    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    console.error(`Failed to load plugin from ${modulePath}: ${errorMessage}`);
    pluginErrors.set(modulePath, errorMessage);
    return null;
  }
}

// Validate module path to prevent dangerous imports
function isValidModulePath(path: string): boolean {
  // Allow HTTPS URLs (e.g., from deno.land, esm.sh, jsr)
  if (path.startsWith("https://")) {
    // Block obviously dangerous patterns
    const blockedPatterns = [
      /[<>"|*?]/,
      /\.\.\//,
    ];
    return !blockedPatterns.some((pattern) => pattern.test(path));
  }

  // Allow jsr: imports
  if (path.startsWith("jsr:")) {
    return true;
  }

  // Allow npm: imports
  if (path.startsWith("npm:")) {
    return true;
  }

  // Block file:// and other protocols for security on Deno Deploy
  if (path.includes("://") && !path.startsWith("https://")) {
    return false;
  }

  // Allow relative paths (for local development)
  if (path.startsWith("./") || path.startsWith("../")) {
    return true;
  }

  return false;
}

// Load plugins from environment variables
async function loadPluginsFromEnv(): Promise<void> {
  // PLUGINS env var: comma-separated list of module paths
  const pluginsEnv = Deno.env.get("PLUGINS");
  if (pluginsEnv) {
    const modulePaths = pluginsEnv.split(",").map((p) => p.trim()).filter((p) => p.length > 0);
    console.log(`Loading ${modulePaths.length} plugin(s) from PLUGINS env var...`);

    const loadPromises = modulePaths.map((path) => loadPlugin(path));
    await Promise.allSettled(loadPromises);
  }

  // Also support individual PLUGIN_* env vars
  // Deno Deploy doesn't support iterating env vars easily,
  // but we can check for numbered plugins
  for (let i = 1; i <= 20; i++) {
    const pluginPath = Deno.env.get(`PLUGIN_${i}`);
    if (pluginPath) {
      await loadPlugin(pluginPath.trim());
    }
  }
}

// Load plugins from query parameters
async function loadPluginsFromQuery(url: URL): Promise<Plugin[]> {
  const loadedPlugins: Plugin[] = [];
  const pluginParams = url.searchParams.getAll("plugin");

  for (const modulePath of pluginParams) {
    const trimmedPath = modulePath.trim();
    if (trimmedPath) {
      const plugin = await loadPlugin(trimmedPath);
      if (plugin) {
        loadedPlugins.push(plugin);
      }
    }
  }

  return loadedPlugins;
}

// Run request through all plugin handlers
async function runPluginHandlers(request: Request): Promise<Response | null> {
  for (const [, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
    if (typeof plugin.handle === "function") {
      try {
        const response = await plugin.handle(request);
        if (response) {
          return response;
        }
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(
          `Plugin ${plugin.name} handler error: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
        );
      }
    }
  }
  return null;
}

// Run data through all plugin transformers
async function runPluginTransformers(data: unknown): Promise<unknown> {
  let result = data;
  for (const [, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
    if (typeof plugin.transform === "function") {
      try {
        result = await plugin.transform(result);
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(
          `Plugin ${plugin.name} transform error: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
        );
      }
    }
  }
  return result;
}

// Get status information about loaded plugins
function getPluginStatus(): Record<string, unknown> {
  const plugins: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];

  for (const [name, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
    plugins.push({
      name,
      version: plugin.version || "unknown",
      hasHandler: typeof plugin.handle === "function",
      hasTransform: typeof plugin.transform === "function",
    });
  }

  const errors: Record<string, string>[] = [];
  for (const [path, error] of pluginErrors) {
    errors.push({ path, error });
  }

  return {
    totalLoaded: pluginRegistry.size,
    totalErrors: pluginErrors.size,
    plugins,
    errors,
  };
}

// JSON response helper
function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
    },
  });
}

// Load environment plugins at startup
let envPluginsLoaded = false;
const envPluginsPromise = loadPluginsFromEnv().then(() => {
  envPluginsLoaded = true;
  console.log(`Startup plugin loading complete. ${pluginRegistry.size} plugin(s) loaded.`);
}).catch((err) => {
  envPluginsLoaded = true;
  console.error("Error during startup plugin loading:", err);
});

// Main request handler
Deno.serve(async (request: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  // Ensure env plugins are loaded before handling requests
  if (!envPluginsLoaded) {
    await envPluginsPromise;
  }

  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const path = url.pathname;

  try {
    // Handle CORS preflight
    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
      return new Response(null, {
        headers: {
          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS",
          "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization",
        },
      });
    }

    // API Routes
    switch (path) {
      case "/": {
        return jsonResponse({
          service: "Deno Deploy Plugin System",
          version: "1.0.0",
          endpoints: {
            "/": "This help message",
            "/status": "Get loaded plugin status",
            "/plugins": "List all loaded plugins",
            "/load": "Load a plugin via query param: ?plugin=<url>",
            "/unload": "Unload a plugin: ?name=<plugin_name>",
            "/execute": "Execute plugin transformers on posted data",
            "/health": "Health check",
          },
          pluginsLoaded: pluginRegistry.size,
        });
      }

      case "/health": {
        return jsonResponse({
          status: "ok",
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
          pluginsLoaded: pluginRegistry.size,
        });
      }

      case "/status": {
        return jsonResponse(getPluginStatus());
      }

      case "/plugins": {
        const plugins: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];
        for (const [name, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
          plugins.push({
            name,
            version: plugin.version || "unknown",
            capabilities: {
              handle: typeof plugin.handle === "function",
              transform: typeof plugin.transform === "function",
              initialize: typeof plugin.initialize === "function",
            },
          });
        }
        return jsonResponse({ count: plugins.length, plugins });
      }

      case "/load": {
        // Load plugins specified via query parameters
        const pluginPaths = url.searchParams.getAll("plugin");
        if (pluginPaths.length === 0) {
          return jsonResponse(
            {
              error: "No plugin specified",
              usage: "/load?plugin=https://example.com/my-plugin.ts",
            },
            400,
          );
        }

        const results: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];
        const newPlugins = await loadPluginsFromQuery(url);

        for (const path of pluginPaths) {
          const trimmedPath = path.trim();
          const loaded = newPlugins.find((p) =>
            p.name === trimmedPath.split("/").pop()?.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, "")
          );
          const error = pluginErrors.get(trimmedPath);

          results.push({
            path: trimmedPath,
            status: loaded ? "loaded" : "failed",
            name: loaded?.name || null,
            error: error || null,
          });
        }

        return jsonResponse({
          message: `Processed ${pluginPaths.length} plugin(s)`,
          results,
          totalPluginsLoaded: pluginRegistry.size,
        });
      }

      case "/unload": {
        const pluginName = url.searchParams.get("name");
        if (!pluginName) {
          return jsonResponse(
            { error: "No plugin name specified", usage: "/unload?name=my-plugin" },
            400,
          );
        }

        if (pluginRegistry.has(pluginName)) {
          pluginRegistry.delete(pluginName);
          return jsonResponse({
            message: `Plugin '${pluginName}' unloaded successfully`,
            totalPluginsLoaded: pluginRegistry.size,
          });
        } else {
          return jsonResponse(
            {
              error: `Plugin '${pluginName}' not found`,
              availablePlugins: Array.from(pluginRegistry.keys()),
            },
            404,
          );
        }
      }

      case "/execute": {
        if (request.method !== "POST") {
          return jsonResponse({ error: "POST method required" }, 405);
        }

        let inputData: unknown;
        const contentType = request.headers.get("content-type") || "";

        if (contentType.includes("application/json")) {
          inputData = await request.json();
        } else {
          inputData = await request.text();
        }

        const transformedData = await runPluginTransformers(inputData);

        return jsonResponse({
          original: inputData,
          transformed: transformedData,
          pluginsApplied: Array.from(pluginRegistry.entries())
            .filter(([, p]) => typeof p.transform === "function")
            .map(([name]) => name),
        });
      }

      default: {
        // Try to handle the request through plugin handlers
        const pluginResponse = await runPluginHandlers(request);
        if (pluginResponse) {
          return pluginResponse;
        }

        return jsonResponse(
          {
            error: "Not Found",
            message: `No handler found for ${path}`,
            hint: "Visit / for available endpoints, or load a plugin that handles this route",
          },
          404,
        );
      }
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Request handling error:", error);
    return jsonResponse(
      {
        error: "Internal Server Error",
        message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      },
      500,
    );
  }
});
